ogTitle: Upper Lansdowne vs Upper Main Arm: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Upper Lansdowne and Upper Main Arm.
heading: Upper Lansdowne vs Upper Main Arm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Upper Lansdowne, New South Wales and Upper Main Arm, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Upper Lansdowne has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Upper Main Arm (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Upper Lansdowne or Upper Main Arm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Upper Lansdowne has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Upper Main Arm scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Upper Lansdowne scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Upper Main Arm scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Upper Lansdowne or Upper Main Arm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Upper Lansdowne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
